parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: When enabled, will be actively sending to the device.

parameters.name: interface
parameters.label: Interface
parameters.description: Select the type of interface to connect to the device with.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Rate
parameters.description: How often data is sent to the device (per second).

parameters.name: fixture
parameters.label: Fixture
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for DMX Fixture inputs, either wired or referenced.

parameters.name: fixture0active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: When active, this Fixture is merged and sent.

parameters.name: fixture0pop
parameters.label: DMX Fixture POPs
parameters.description: A reference to one or more DMX Fixture POPs. The parameter is disabled if there is a corresponding wired input.

parameters.name: serialport
parameters.label: Serial Port
parameters.description: When the Interface parameter is set to Generic Serial this parameter lets you select which Serial (COM) port to use.

parameters.name: dmxkingport
parameters.label: DMXking Port
parameters.description: Select a DMXking port to send to.

parameters.name: device
parameters.label: Device
parameters.description: Select a DMX device from the menu.

parameters.name: multicast
parameters.label: Multicast
parameters.description: Enable multicast for sACN. Multicast automatically builds the IP based on Net, Subnet, and Universe of the device. This allows for sending to multiple devices at once by specifying multiple universes.

parameters.name: netaddress
parameters.label: Network Address
parameters.description: Specify the IP address to use. This address corresponds to the receiving device address.  When the address is set to its default 255.255.255.255, the messages are instead broadcast to all devices on t

parameters.name: localaddress
parameters.label: Local Address
parameters.description: When the sending machine is equipped with multiple network adapters, this parameter can be used to choose which adapter to send the data from by specifying its IP address here.

parameters.name: localport
parameters.label: Local Port
parameters.description: In rare cases it can be necessary to supply a custom port from which the data should be sent. The default of means the O/S assigned port is being used.

parameters.name: customport
parameters.label: Use Custom Port
parameters.description: Enable the Network Port parameter to specify the port of the receiving hardware. When disabled, default port is 6454 for Art-Net, 5568 for sACN, and 6038 for KiNET.

parameters.name: netport
parameters.label: Network Port
parameters.description: Specify the port of the receiving hardware.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.label: Priority
parameters.description: The data priority, if there are multiple sources. Used with sACN and KiNET protocols.

parameters.name: sendartsync
parameters.label: Send ArtSync
parameters.description: When enabled will send out ArtSync packets. ArtSync packets are used to synchronize multiple universes together. It will do this by waiting for all ArtDmx packets to finish sending, then follow up by

parameters.name: cid
parameters.label: CID
parameters.description: The unique ID of the sender.

parameters.name: source
parameters.label: Source
parameters.description: User assigned name of source (for informative purposes).

parameters.name: kinetversion
parameters.label: KiNET Version
parameters.description: Select the version of the KiNET protocol to use.

parameters.name: customkinetport
parameters.label: Use Custom KiNET Port
parameters.description: When enabled, can specify a custom port for the KiNET v2 interface. When disabled, the port will be the broadcast port: 255.

parameters.name: kinetport
parameters.label: KiNET Port
parameters.description: Specifies the port for KiNET v2 interface.

parameters.name: routingtable
parameters.label: Routing Table
parameters.description: Use a to specify netaddress, source, cid, priority, and kinetport values for specific universes. The net, subnet, universe columns must be filled in with valid values. Note: the can override the netad

parameters.name: bypass
parameters.label: Bypass
parameters.description: Pass through the first input to the output unchanged.

parameters.name: freeextragpumem
parameters.label: Free Extra GPU Memory
parameters.description: Free memory that has accumulated when output memory has grown and shrunk.

parameters.name: delinputattrs
parameters.label: Delete Input Attributes
parameters.description: Only output which attributes you specify in this helps isolate attributes into a separate branch.

examples.label: Configure Active
examples.description: Common settings from 2 real Derivative examples
